aabbccddeeff112233445566080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
aabbccddeeff112233445566080045000028123440004011dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00140001000000005018ffff00000000
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd600000000014064020010db800000000000000000000000120010db8000000000000000000000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd600000000014114020010db800000000000000000000000120010db8000000000000000000000002303901bb00140001000000005018ffff00000000
# --- rung 1: VLAN / MPLS (upstream bpf_flow.c semantics) ---
# accept: single 802.1Q (vid 100) + IPv4/TCP
aabbccddeeff11223344556681000064080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# accept: single 802.1Q (vid 100) + IPv6/UDP
aabbccddeeff1122334455668100006486dd600000000014114020010db800000000000000000000000120010db8000000000000000000000002303901bb00140001000000005018ffff00000000
# accept: 802.1AD (vid 100) + 802.1Q (vid 101) + IPv4/TCP (QinQ)
aabbccddeeff11223344556688a8006481000065080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# accept: MPLS unicast, one label (label 100, S=1, ttl 64) — kernel stops here
aabbccddeeff11223344556688470006414045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# accept: 802.1Q + MPLS unicast — VLAN advances offsets, MPLS stops
aabbccddeeff1122334455668100006488470006414045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# drop: Q-in-Q with two 0x8100 tags (kernel forbids double-Q)
aabbccddeeff1122334455668100006481000065080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# drop: 802.1AD tag after a 802.1Q tag
aabbccddeeff1122334455668100006488a80065080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# drop: 802.1AD not followed by 802.1Q (IPv4 directly)
aabbccddeeff11223344556688a80064080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# drop: triple tag AD+Q+Q
aabbccddeeff11223344556688a800648100006581000066080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# drop: unknown ethertype (ARP)
aabbccddeeff112233445566080600010800060400011122334455660a000001aabbccddeeff0a000002
# --- rung 1 follow-up: coverage growth (review-recommended; all expected to agree) ---
# accept: MPLS multicast (0x8848), one label — proves the 0x8848 arm
aabbccddeeff11223344556688480006414045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# drop: 802.1Q + ARP inner — parse_vlan_q default reject == kernel parse_eth_proto default drop
aabbccddeeff11223344556681000064080600010800060400011122334455660a000001aabbccddeeff0a000002
# drop: truncated 802.1Q tag (2 of 4 bytes) — extract-fail == kernel header-read fail
aabbccddeeff11223344556681000064
# accept: 802.1AD + 802.1Q + IPv6/UDP — QinQ with v6 inner
aabbccddeeff11223344556688a800648100006586dd600000000014114020010db800000000000000000000000120010db8000000000000000000000002303901bb00140001000000005018ffff00000000
# drop: truncated MPLS entry (2 of 4 bytes)
aabbccddeeff11223344556688470006
# --- rung 2: IPv6 extension-header chain (default flags) ---
# accept: IPv6 + HopByHop(1 opt, nexthdr=TCP) + TCP — minimal single loop iter
# (byte-identical to project_tests::projects_ipv6_hopopt_tcp)
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd60000000001c004020010db800000000000000000000000120010db80000000000000000000000020600000000000000303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# accept: IPv6 + DestOpts + HopByHop + UDP — two options (self-loop twice), ip_proto=17 from last link
# (byte-identical to project_tests::projects_ipv6_two_opts_udp)
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd6000000000183c4020010db800000000000000000000000120010db800000000000000000000000200000000000000001100000000000000303901bb00080000
# accept: IPv6 + Fragment (first frag, offset 0) — stops; is_frag=is_first_frag=true, ports 0
# (byte-identical to project_tests::projects_ipv6_frag_first)
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd6000000000082c4020010db800000000000000000000000120010db80000000000000000000000020600000000000001
# accept: IPv6 + Fragment (later frag, offset!=0) — stops; is_frag=true, is_first_frag=false
# (byte-identical to project_tests::projects_ipv6_frag_later)
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd6000000000082c4020010db800000000000000000000000120010db80000000000000000000000020600000900000001
# accept: IPv6 + HopByHop + Fragment — option then frag terminal
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd600000000010004020010db800000000000000000000000120010db80000000000000000000000022c000000000000000600000000000001
# accept: IPv6 non-zero flow_label + TCP — flow_label recorded, no early stop
# (byte-identical to project_tests::projects_ipv6_flow_label)
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd600123450014064020010db800000000000000000000000120010db8000000000000000000000002303901bb00000001000000005018ffff00000000
# drop: IPv6 + HopByHop truncated (hdrlen claims 16-byte option header, only 8 bytes present) — extract-fail on the following TCP read == kernel drop (downstream get_header)
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd600000000008004020010db800000000000000000000000120010db80000000000000000000000020601000000000000
# drop: IPv6 + option chain final nexthdr unsupported (89/OSPF) — parse_ip_proto default BPF_DROP
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd600000000008004020010db800000000000000000000000120010db80000000000000000000000025900000000000000
# --- rung 3: TCP options (doff-sized region) ---
# accept: IPv4 + TCP doff=6 (+4 option bytes, MSS 0204 05b4) — options fit, kernel reads ports
aabbccddeeff11223344556608004500002c123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000006018ffff00000000020405b4
# accept: IPv6 + TCP doff=6 (+4 option bytes, MSS) — v6 arm
aabbccddeeff11223344556686dd600000000018064020010db800000000000000000000000120010db8000000000000000000000002303901bb00000001000000006018ffff00000000020405b4
# drop: IPv4 + TCP doff=4 (<5) — kernel `tcp->doff < 5` DROP == our wrapped-length oob reject
aabbccddeeff112233445566080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000004018ffff00000000
# drop: IPv4 + TCP doff=6 but no option bytes present — kernel `tcp+doff*4 > data_end` DROP == our truncation
aabbccddeeff112233445566080045000028123440004006dead0a0000010a000002303901bb00000001000000006018ffff00000000
